summary: The Docker-based reproduction successfully demonstrates that a malicious MCPB package with path traversal in the manifest name field can write files outside the intended upload directory. The PoC creates a proof-of-concept file at /tmp/pwned-by-mcpb/proof.txt, confirming arbitrary file write capability.
expected_security_impact: Uploading a malicious MCPB package should cause files from the archive to be extracted outside the data/uploads/mcpb directory, specifically to /tmp/pwned-by-mcpb/proof.txt, demonstrating arbitrary file write through path traversal.
actual_observed_result: Running ./reproduction/run.sh successfully built the MCPHub container, started the server with skipAuth enabled, uploaded the malicious MCPB package, and verified that the proof.txt file was written to /tmp/pwned-by-mcpb/proof.txt outside the upload directory, confirming the arbitrary file write vulnerability.
verification_method: The verification script checks for the existence of /tmp/pwned-by-mcpb/proof.txt after upload and compares its contents with the expected proof string. This distinguishes successful path traversal exploitation from normal upload behavior where files would remain inside data/uploads/mcpb/.
evidence_produced_by_run: Container logs show successful MCPB file upload and processing
The file /tmp/pwned-by-mcpb/proof.txt exists after the upload attempt
The proof.txt file contains the expected content 'ARBITRARY_FILE_WRITE_SUCCESS', confirming the attacker-controlled file was written to the target location
The normal upload directory data/uploads/mcpb/ does not contain the proof.txt file, confirming it was written outside the intended boundary

reproduction.source_acquisition: run.sh clones the target repository on the host, checks out the pinned vulnerable commit, and mounts that checkout into the container at /target-repo.
